name: the-brain-that-changes-itself
description: Norman Doidge's 'The Brain That Changes Itself: Stories of Personal Triumph from the Frontiers of Brain Science' — the landmark book on neuroplasticity. Through stories of patients and scientists, Doidge shows that the brain can rewire itself at any age: stroke victims recover, learning disorders are cured, blind people learn to see, and OCD is treated by changing thought patterns.
useCase: Understanding neuroplasticity, learning how the brain rewires itself, stroke and brain injury recovery, treating OCD and chronic pain, improving cognitive function, and preserving brain health in aging.
knownRisks: Published in 2007 — some research has advanced since
Clinical results vary — not all patients respond to these treatments
Self-directed neuroplasticity requires significant effort and discipline
Not all conditions covered have equal scientific support
